channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Justice Department withheld and removed some Epstein files related to Trump
item.description: An NPR investigation finds the public database of Epstein files is missing dozens of pages related to sexual abuse accusations against President Trump...

item.title: These small business owners are owed tariff refunds. Will they ever get them?
item.description: Anyone who paid the taxes should get reimbursed, but the high court did not address how. Business owners wonder if they'll need lawyers, brokers, mone...

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump to deliver first State of the Union address of his second term, what's next for Mexico after killing of cartel leader, NPR investigation finds D...

item.title: State of the Union May Be a Trial for ‘Barely Invited’ Justices
item.description: If tradition holds, members of the Supreme Court will attend Trump’s annual speech, just days after ruling against the legality of his tariffs.

item.title: Why Gavin Newsom refuses to be a "bystander" in this political moment
item.description: What does the Democratic leader see for himself in the years to come?

item.title: 'Fear of Flying Clinic' helps anxious travelers back into the skies
item.description: For 50 years, a San Francisco-based group has created a space where fearful flyers can get supported exposure to air travel.

item.title: What to Know in NYC After Blizzard: School Closures, Transportation and More
item.description: Public schools and Broadway shows will be open, but transit and flight disruptions could linger.

item.title: How Russia Put Its Future at Risk by Remaking Its Economy for War
item.description: About half of the country’s federal budget goes toward the fight in Ukraine, money that does little to support its long-term development.

item.title: China Amps Up Pressure on Japan With Restrictions on Exports
item.description: Beijing placed the restrictions on 20 Japanese entities with ties to the defense industry, the latest ratcheting up of its monthslong feud with Tokyo.

item.title: How Former Prince Andrew’s Ties to Epstein Led to His Downfall
item.description: Last week, the British authorities arrested the former Prince Andrew amid allegations that he shared confidential government information with Jeffrey ...

item.title: Is It Safe to Travel to Mexico Right Now, Given the Cartel Violence?
item.description: A wave of unrest after the killing of a cartel leader has rattled tourists at popular destinations in Mexico, prompting travelers to reconsider their ...

item.title: China Amps Up Pressure on Japan With Restrictions on Exports
item.description: Beijing placed the restrictions on 20 Japanese entities with ties to the defense industry, the latest ratcheting up of its monthslong feud with Tokyo.

item.title: Record Snowfall Slams New England and Leaves New York City Snarled
item.description: At the storm’s peak, well over half a million homes and businesses in the Northeast were without power.

item.title: How Bookbinders Helped the Nazis Track Holocaust Victims
item.description: Conservation experts helped the Nazi regime inspect church and civil archives to track down people they sought to persecute, a researcher concluded.

item.title: How Russia Put Its Future at Risk by Remaking Its Economy for War
item.description: About half of the country’s federal budget goes toward the fight in Ukraine, money that does little to support its long-term development.
